geology.name: Sambangsan Formation
geology.description: Contains structural deformation from faults and folds, with high-density joints and fracture zones, and developed foliation showing anisotropy. Uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) is typically 50-100 MPa when fresh, but can drop below 20 MPa in shale sections.

geology.name: Imgye Granite
geology.description: Based on Precambrian granitic gneiss with Cretaceous biotite granite intrusion, showing anisotropy according to micro-fracture direction and high weathering resistance. Many sections have high strength with uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) of 100-200 MPa or more.

geology.name: Jeongseon Limestone
geology.description: Upper strata of the Ordovician Joseon Supergroup from the Paleozoic era. Includes limestone and partially dolomite layers. Uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) is generally 80-150 MPa.

geology.name: Nokan Series
geology.description: Composed of green to gray-green fine-grained sandstone, siltstone, and shale. Uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) is 80-120 MPa in fresh sandstone and 40-70 MPa in mixed shale and siltstone sections, vulnerable to moisture.

geology.name: Recent River Deposits
geology.description: Mixture of gravel, sand, silt, and some clay transported from upper river reaches, primarily composed of gravel and sand, resulting in very high permeability coefficient.

geology.name: Biotite Gneiss
geology.description: A type of metamorphic rock, forming the basement rock that occupies a significant portion of the Korean Peninsula ground. When fresh, uniaxial compressive strength (UCS) is 100-200 MPa. When weathered, it drops to 20-50 MPa or below.

landuse.name: Residential Area
landuse.description: A densely populated area with residential buildings such as single-family houses and apartments, along with their associated facilities (yards, parking lots, etc.).

landuse.name: Transportation Area
landuse.description: Facilities and sites for transportation systems installed for the movement of people or cargo, including roads, railways, airports, and ports.

landuse.name: Public Facility Area
landuse.description: Facilities and their associated sites operated for public benefit, including schools, government offices, hospitals, and religious facilities.

landuse.name: Paddy Field
landuse.description: A type of paddy field where water is retained and managed for a certain period for rice cultivation.

landuse.name: Dry Field
landuse.description: A typical upland cropland where vegetables, grains, and specialty crops are cultivated without water retention.

landuse.name: Greenhouse Cultivation Area
landuse.description: An area where crops are cultivated inside facilities such as plastic greenhouses or glasshouses that can control temperature and humidity.

landuse.name: Other Cultivation Area
landuse.description: Fixed-form agricultural land that does not fall under paddy fields, dry fields, or greenhouse cultivation, such as orchards, nurseries, and ginseng fields.

landuse.name: Broadleaf Forest
landuse.description: A forest area where broad-leaved trees (oak, maple, etc.) predominate, typically deciduous with seasonal leaf fall.

landuse.name: Coniferous Forest
landuse.description: A forest area where needle-leaved trees such as pine, Korean pine, and fir predominate.

landuse.name: Mixed Forest
landuse.description: A forest area where broad-leaved and coniferous trees grow mixed together without any single species dominating.

landuse.name: Natural Grassland
landuse.description: Grassland naturally occurring in mountainous areas or plains, covered with grasses and plants, with minimal artificial management.

landuse.name: Artificial Grassland
landuse.description: Grassland artificially created and managed for specific purposes, such as golf courses, soccer fields, ranches, and park lawns.

landuse.name: Inland Wetland
landuse.description: A wet area such as swamps or marshes around rivers or lakes that remains waterlogged year-round or for extended periods.

landuse.name: Artificial Bare Land
landuse.description: Land where vegetation has been removed and the ground surface is exposed due to human activities, such as construction sites, vacant lots, landfills, and quarries.

landuse.name: Inland Water
landuse.description: An area located within land where water surfaces are permanently exposed, including rivers, lakes, reservoirs, and dams.

soil_texture.name: Loamy skeletal
soil_texture.description: The fine soil is loam, but more than 35% by volume consists of gravel or rock fragments, resulting in very rapid drainage and good bearing capacity.

soil_texture.name: Coarse loamy
soil_texture.description: A coarse loamy soil with relatively high sand content and low clay content, with good aeration and moderate water retention capacity.

soil_texture.name: Fine loamy
soil_texture.description: A fine loamy soil with appropriate clay content providing cohesiveness, excellent water and nutrient retention, favorable for vegetation growth.

soil_texture.name: Fine loamy
soil_texture.description: A fine loamy soil with appropriate clay content providing cohesiveness, excellent water and nutrient retention, favorable for vegetation growth.

soil_texture.name: Sandy skeletal
soil_texture.description: The fine soil is primarily sand, with more than 35% gravel mixed in, resulting in very high permeability and weak bonding strength.

soil_texture.name: Rock
soil_texture.description: Soil is barely developed or bedrock is exposed above the ground surface, making it difficult for vegetation to grow and having low permeability.

soil_texture.name: Coarse loamy/sandy skeletal
soil_texture.description: A soil with very coarse particle size distribution, formed by layers or mixtures of coarse loamy soil and sandy soil with many rock fragments.

soil_texture.name: Fine silty/coarse silt
soil_texture.description: A mixture of very fine and coarse silt, providing a smooth texture but highly vulnerable to water erosion.

soil_texture.name: Gravelly Clay Loam
soil_texture.description: A clay loam soil mixed with sand and containing gravel, providing both cohesiveness and drainage capacity.

soil_texture.name: Clayey skeletal
soil_texture.description: A structure where more than 35% rock fragments are embedded within fine soil with high clay content, which may reduce shear strength when wet.

soil_texture.name: Loamy skeletal
soil_texture.description: The fine soil is loam, but more than 35% by volume consists of gravel or rock fragments, resulting in very rapid drainage and good bearing capacity.

soil_texture.name: Coarse loamy/sandy
soil_texture.description: A barren soil with coarse loamy soil mixed with sand, showing almost no cohesiveness and very rapid water drainage.

soil_texture.name: Fine clayey
soil_texture.description: Very high clay content with fine and dense particles, excellent water retention but poor drainage, prone to cracking when dry.

soil_sub_texture.name: Loamy sand
soil_sub_texture.description: Sand is the main component with some silt or clay mixed in, resulting in very rapid drainage and weak inter-particle bonding.

soil_sub_texture.name: Clay loam
soil_sub_texture.description: Considerable clay content makes it sticky when wet and very hard when dry, with excellent nutrient and water retention.

soil_sub_texture.name: Sandy loam
soil_sub_texture.description: A typical soil with high sand content, good aeration, appropriate drainage, and easy cultivation and excavation.

soil_sub_texture.name: Gravelly loam
soil_sub_texture.description: Gravel mixed within loam provides better drainage than typical loam and relatively stable ground bearing capacity.

soil_sub_texture.name: Rock
soil_sub_texture.description: Bedrock exposure with barely developed soil layers, making vegetation growth difficult and having very low permeability.

soil_sub_texture.name: Fine silty loam
soil_sub_texture.description: Composed of very fine silt particles with excellent water retention but highly vulnerable to erosion by water flow.

soil_sub_texture.name: Gravelly sandy loam
soil_sub_texture.description: A coarse-textured soil primarily composed of sand and gravel, with very rapid drainage, almost no cohesiveness, and prone to collapse.

soil_sub_texture.name: Loam
soil_sub_texture.description: A fertile soil with balanced mixture of sand, silt, and clay, providing both excellent water retention and aeration.

soil_sub_texture.name: Very gravelly loam
soil_sub_texture.description: Loam filled with more than 35-60% gravel by volume, where the skeletal characteristics of gravel rather than soil determine ground strength.

soil_sub_texture.name: Cobbly clay
soil_sub_texture.description: Clayey soil mixed with rounded cobbles, with very high viscosity and strong resistance to heavy machinery operations.

soil_sub_texture.name: Very gravelly clay loam
soil_sub_texture.description: A robust ground with dense mixture of clay and gravel, having low permeability but a hard skeletal structure.

soil_sub_texture.name: Gravelly silty clay loam
soil_sub_texture.description: A form with gravel included in fine silt/clay material, highly sticky yet with some drainage channels secured internally.

soil_sub_texture.name: Silty clay loam
soil_sub_texture.description: Very high silt and clay content with fine particles, holding much water but poor drainage, making it prone to becoming soft.

soil_series.name: CsF
soil_series.description: Mountainous terrain soil derived from limestone, with gravel-rich loam and steep slopes, primarily used as forest land.

soil_series.name: WjE
soil_series.description: Brown forest soil developed from limestone residual layers, with excellent drainage, primarily distributed in forest areas.

soil_series.name: SiD
soil_series.description: Mountainous soil derived from metamorphic rocks (gneiss, etc.), with sandy loam texture, suitable for forest vegetation.

soil_series.name: SiC
soil_series.description: Mountainous soil derived from metamorphic rocks (gneiss, etc.), with sandy loam texture, suitable for forest vegetation.

soil_series.name: ChE
soil_series.description: Silty clay loam distributed in valleys and alluvial fans of limestone regions, primarily used as dry fields.

soil_series.name: MuD
soil_series.description: Dark brown soil derived from limestone, with high gravel content, located on mountainous slopes.

soil_series.name: UgE
soil_series.description: Coarse loamy soil developed from mountainous residual layers, a forest soil with high forestry value.

soil_series.name: SIE
soil_series.description: Mountainous soil derived from metamorphic rocks (gneiss, etc.), with sandy loam texture, suitable for forest vegetation.

soil_series.name: ImB
soil_series.description: Distributed on high-altitude flat surfaces or gentle slopes, with high clay content clay loam, commonly used for highland vegetable fields.

soil_series.name: ImC
soil_series.description: Distributed on high-altitude flat surfaces or gentle slopes, with high clay content clay loam, commonly used for highland vegetable fields.

soil_series.name: Ng
soil_series.description: A very gravel-rich soil in mountainous terrain, with rapid drainage and severe surface exposure, making it barren.

soil_series.name: Hr
soil_series.description: Gravelly alluvial layer soil along rivers, with very high permeability, somewhat barren for agricultural use.

soil_series.name: RC
soil_series.description: An area with barely developed soil and exposed bedrock, with sparse vegetation.

soil_series.name: Dq
soil_series.description: Brown soil developed in valleys of limestone regions, with good drainage, suitable for dry field farming.

soil_series.name: Gt
soil_series.description: Coarse loamy soil derived from mountainous weathered layers, primarily distributed in steep-sloped forest areas.

soil_series.name: WjF
soil_series.description: Brown forest soil developed from limestone residual layers, with excellent drainage, primarily distributed in forest areas.

soil_series.name: MuC
soil_series.description: Dark brown soil derived from limestone, with high gravel content, located on mountainous slopes.

soil_series.name: SlB
soil_series.description: Located in plateau areas or gentle slopes, a loamy soil with relatively high organic matter content.

soil_series.name: SpC
soil_series.description: Sandy loam soil developed on flatlands near rivers, with high utility for agriculture and residential use.

soil_series.name: SpB
soil_series.description: Sandy loam soil developed on flatlands near rivers, with high utility for agriculture and residential use.

soil_series.name: OdF
soil_series.description: Brown forest soil developed in high mountain areas, characterized by low temperatures and high moisture.

soil_series.name: Gz
soil_series.description: Distributed in flat sedimentary terrain of river basins, a gravelly soil with very rapid drainage.

soil_series.name: HgB
soil_series.description: Gravel-mixed loamy soil derived from river alluvial layers, primarily utilized as dry fields or orchards.

soil_series.name: UgC
soil_series.description: Coarse loamy soil developed from mountainous residual layers, a forest soil with high forestry value.

soil_series.name: OdE
soil_series.description: Brown forest soil developed in high mountain areas, characterized by low temperatures and high moisture.

soil_series.name: ChD
soil_series.description: Silty clay loam distributed in valleys and alluvial fans of limestone regions, primarily used as dry fields.

soil_series.name: SoD
soil_series.description: Forest soil derived from metamorphic rock residual layers, with deep soil depth and loam structure mixed with gravel.

soil_series.name: PuB
soil_series.description: Sandy soil with high sand content near rivers, with very high permeability and weak bonding strength.

soil_series.name: CsE
soil_series.description: Mountainous terrain soil derived from limestone, with gravel-rich loam and steep slopes, primarily used as forest land.

soil_series.name: DEC
soil_series.description: A typical plateau field soil developed in cold climates of high-altitude flat surfaces.

soil_series.name: RL
soil_series.description: Recent riverbed deposits on river bottoms or floodplains, consisting of sand and gravel layers constantly affected by water.

soil_series.name: DED
soil_series.description: A typical plateau field soil developed in cold climates of high-altitude flat surfaces.

soil_series.name: DEB
soil_series.description: A typical plateau field soil developed in cold climates of high-altitude flat surfaces.

soil_series.name: JsF
soil_series.description: A unique mountainous soil derived from limestone in the Jeongseon region, characterized by high gravel content and extreme barrenness.

soil_series.name: PaC
soil_series.description: Reddish clay loam soil (Terra Rossa series) on alluvial fans of limestone regions, widely used for dry field farming.

soil_series.name: MtD
soil_series.description: A soil distributed on steep slopes with weak soil layer development and very abundant rock fragments.

soil_series.name: JsE
soil_series.description: A unique mountainous soil derived from limestone in the Jeongseon region, characterized by high gravel content and extreme barrenness.

soil_series.name: MtE
soil_series.description: A soil distributed on steep slopes with weak soil layer development and very abundant rock fragments.

soil_series.name: HgC
soil_series.description: Gravel-mixed loamy soil derived from river alluvial layers, primarily utilized as dry fields or orchards.

soil_series.name: KJB
soil_series.description: A loamy sandy soil series on flatlands around rivers, a sedimentary soil with excellent drainage.

soil_series.name: MjB
soil_series.description: Sedimentary soil in valleys of metamorphic rock regions, relatively fertile and utilized as paddy fields or dry fields.

soil_series.name: MjC
soil_series.description: Sedimentary soil in valleys of metamorphic rock regions, relatively fertile and utilized as paddy fields or dry fields.

soil_series.name: PaD
soil_series.description: Reddish clay loam soil (Terra Rossa series) on alluvial fans of limestone regions, widely used for dry field farming.

soil_series.name: MtC
soil_series.description: A soil distributed on steep slopes with weak soil layer development and very abundant rock fragments.

soil_series.name: AmB
soil_series.description: Brown soil from mountainous terrain residual layers, distributed in forest areas with well-preserved natural forests.

soil_series.name: MoC
soil_series.description: Coarse-textured soil in hilly or mountainous areas, with rapid drainage and relatively low water retention.

soil_series.name: Bo
soil_series.description: Clay loam soil in alluvial plains, with somewhat poor drainage, primarily used as paddy fields.

soil_series.name: NkB
soil_series.description: Sandy alluvial soil widely distributed along riverbanks, with very high sand content.

soil_series.name: GpB
soil_series.description: Sandy loam soil on gentle slopes, with good aeration, suitable for vegetable or field crop cultivation.

soil_series.name: MuE
soil_series.description: Dark brown soil derived from limestone, with high gravel content, located on mountainous slopes.

soil_series.name: HjB
soil_series.description: Reddish-brown clay loam soil in low hills of limestone regions, with relatively deep soil depth.

soil_series.name: Jd
soil_series.description: Recent sedimentary soil along rivers, with many coarse particles, limiting agricultural utilization.

soil_series.name: OeB
soil_series.description: Developed on gentle slopes at the base of mountains, showing good drainage and stable ground characteristics.

soil_series.name: GbD
soil_series.description: Soil derived from red shale or metamorphic rocks, a clay loam with somewhat high clay content.

soil_thickness.description: Relatively shallow soil depth, with bedrock close to the surface, which may limit root development of large plant species.
Soil depth exceeds 1m, very favorable for vegetation growth, and forms long stable soil sections during ground excavation.
A section with average soil depth, providing appropriate soil depth for most crops and trees to grow.
Very shallow soil depth with bedrock almost exposed, low water retention, unfavorable for vegetation establishment.
Rock outcrop or water body with no soil present, geotechnically a complete bedrock section.

soil_drainage.name: Somewhat excessive
soil_drainage.description: Rapid water drainage makes it prone to drought damage, primarily found in sandy or gravel-rich soils.

soil_drainage.name: Well
soil_drainage.description: The most ideal balance between water retention and drainage, optimal for both vegetation growth and civil engineering construction.

soil_drainage.name: Moderately well
soil_drainage.description: Generally well-drained, but temporary water stagnation may occur during rainfall due to characteristics of lower layers.

soil_drainage.name: Very poorly
soil_drainage.description: Almost no water drainage, constantly waterlogged or with very high groundwater level, likely to be soft ground.

soil_drainage.name: Imperfectly
soil_drainage.description: Somewhat slow drainage tends to maintain excessive moisture in the soil, and artificial drainage facilities are recommended.

forest_density.name: C
forest_density.description: Canopy coverage area of trees is 71% or more

forest_density.name: B
forest_density.description: Canopy coverage area of trees is 51% to 70%

forest_density.name: A
forest_density.description: Canopy coverage area of trees is 50% or less

forest_age.name: No forest
forest_age.description: An area where trees with measurable age are absent.

forest_age.name: 1st age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_age.name: 2nd age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_age.name: 3rd age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_age.name: 4th age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_age.name: 5th age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_age.name: 6th age class
forest_age.description: Forest age classified in 10-year units, representing maturity from 1st age class (1-10 years) to 6th age class (51 years or more).

forest_diameter.name: Sapling
forest_diameter.description: Forest stand where trees with DBH less than 6cm account for 50% or more of canopy coverage

forest_diameter.name: Small diameter
forest_diameter.description: Forest stand where trees with DBH 6-16cm account for 50% or more of canopy coverage

forest_diameter.name: Medium diameter
forest_diameter.description: Forest stand where trees with DBH 18-28cm account for 50% or more of canopy coverage

forest_diameter.name: Large diameter
forest_diameter.description: Forest stand where trees with DBH 30cm or more account for 50% or more of canopy coverage

forest_type.name: Pine forest
forest_type.description: A coniferous forest where pine trees predominate.

forest_type.name: Non-forest
forest_type.description: Land currently used as cropland or for non-forest purposes.

forest_type.name: Larch forest
forest_type.description: A coniferous forest where larch trees predominate.

forest_type.name: Korean pine forest
forest_type.description: A coniferous forest where Korean pine trees predominate.

forest_type.name: Cultivated land
forest_type.description: Land currently used as cropland or for non-forest purposes.

forest_type.name: Coniferous-broadleaf mixed forest
forest_type.description: A forest where coniferous and broad-leaved trees are mixed without any single species dominating.

forest_type.name: Poplar forest
forest_type.description: A forest where specific tree species such as poplar or paper mulberry form communities.

forest_type.name: Broadleaf forest
forest_type.description: A forest where broad-leaved trees grow naturally or have been artificially established.

forest_type.name: Paper mulberry forest
forest_type.description: A forest where specific tree species such as poplar or paper mulberry form communities.

forest_type.name: Broadleaf artificial forest
forest_type.description: A forest where broad-leaved trees grow naturally or have been artificially established.
